response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and D language — with no meaningful Java or Kotlin work visible in the resume and zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience. The Java entries that appear (robotic arms at Altom, NI LabWindows/CVI) are either brief or C/C++-focused, and none demonstrate JVM-depth or enterprise framework knowledge. While he has a genuine background in developer tooling and software craftsmanship, the core technical requirements for this role are simply absent.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling background: built test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators, and contributed to a C-based IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing) across multiple roles
Broad systems experience spanning embedded, mobile, desktop, and web — demonstrates adaptability and architectural range
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin expertise; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and D — Java appears only as a peripheral tool in two roles
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume
No exposure to JVM internals, build tooling (Maven/Gradle), or application server integration required for this role

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and IDE internals exposure
